overview.description: The State Street SPDR Nuveen ICE Municipal Bond ETF (TFI) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the ICE AMT-Free US Select Municipal index. The fund tracks a market-value-weighted index of long-term tax-exempt US municipal bonds. TFI was launched on Sep 11, 2007 and is issued by State Street.
infoTable: ICE AMT-Free US Select Municipal Index

data.title: Boomer Retirement Wave Impacts Muni Bonds
data.text: By some estimates, 11,000 baby boomers retire each and every day. For those keeping score at home, that works out to be 4.1 million boomers leaving the traditional workforce every year.

data.title: All eyes are now on the bond market as oil prices fall. Will the Fed hike rates?
data.text: The $30 Treasury market is taking a wait-and-see approach to the U.S.-Iran peace framework deal and to Kevin Warsh's first meeting as Federal Reserve chair.

data.text: Bloomberg's Aashah Shah joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Real Yield." May has been a strong month for muni bond issuance, with about $35 billion of sales so far this year.

data.title: Opinion | You're Probably Overinvested in Bonds
data.text: The usual advice is to hold only 60% of your assets in stock. If you're wealthy, a 90/10 split is far better.
